state.title: Redesign merge state detection across resolve-conflicts, review gate, and diff capture
state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: BACKGROUND: The merge state detection ecosystem spans 5+ components patched independently over several iterations, creating a fragile multi-layer workaround stack. Components: (1) resolve-conflicts skill uses git diff --diff-filter=U to detect conflicts — exits early when auto-resolve stages files but MERGE_HEAD still exists. (2) pre-commit-review-gate.sh detects MERGE_HEAD and filters incoming-only files via merge-base. (3) compute-diff-hash.sh detects MERGE_HEAD to scope diff to worktree-only changes. (4) capture-review-diff.sh same MERGE_HEAD detection. (5) merge-to-main.sh _auto_resolve_archive_conflicts stages files so --diff-filter=U returns empty while rebase is still in progress.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: HISTORY OF FIXES (oscillation pattern): d4a224c1 added MERGE_HEAD exemption to review-gate. 676ca3b0 prohibited blind --ours in resolve-conflicts, fixed review gate merge-base filtering. 892565fa fixed compute-diff-hash and capture-review-diff to scope diffs during merges. fce83734 added cross-worktree merge commit verification. 70d51bcd added .test-index auto-prune during merges. Each fix addressed a downstream symptom rather than the root detection mechanism.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CORE PROBLEM: --diff-filter=U (unmerged files) is used as a proxy for 'merge in progress with conflicts' but these are orthogonal: MERGE_HEAD exists = merge in progress, --diff-filter=U non-empty = unresolved conflicts. After auto-resolution, MERGE_HEAD still exists but --diff-filter=U is empty. Components have interdependent assumptions about when MERGE_HEAD exists and what --diff-filter=U means. PROPOSED APPROACH: Design a unified merge-state API that all components consume, centralizing 'in merge', 'has conflicts', and 'resolved but not committed' into one authoritative source. RELATED: dso-vcrq, 1ded-89e6, a45c-400e.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
